name: D3 Ch12 — Xil City Encounters, Main Thoroughfare (1-in-6)
description: City encounter table for Xil's main streets, the Great Bazaar, and near noble estates, Chapter 12 of D3. Encounter frequency: 1 in 6 per new district entered. Roll 1d20 on this table. Noble house retinue: roll 1d8 (1-2=Veythrae, 3=Xilrath, 4=Zhaelor, 5=Dravun, 6=Thalmyr, 7=Velouryn, 8=Vorryn or Draethi).

results.text: Drow patrol, mixed (2 male fighters HD 3+3, 1 patrol leader HD 5+5 + 4–8 quaggoths; XPV 175/400/100 each)
Drow patrol, male (7–10 fighters HD 3+3 hp 22, 1 patrol leader HD 5+5 hp 40, 1 patrol commander HD 7+7 hp 56; XPV 175/400/850)
Drow patrol, female (7–10 fighters HD 3+3 hp 22, 1 patrol leader HD 5+5 hp 40, 1 patrol commander — Level 6 cleric with full spells; XPV 175/400/850+)
Noble house retinue (roll 1d8: 1–2=Veythrae, 3=Xilrath, 4=Zhaelor, 5=Dravun, 6=Thalmyr, 7=Velouryn, 8=Vorryn or Draethi; 4–6 fighters, 1 noble HD 5+5, 6–8 quaggoth bearers)
Merchants with guards (3–4 drow merchants, 6 fighter escorts HD 3+3, 8–12 slave bearers; Velouryn-affiliated)
Slave coffle (20–40 mixed humanoids chained, 4–6 bugbear handlers HD 3+1, 1 drow overseer HD 3+3)
Priestess procession of Lolth (4–8 priestesses Level 2–4 cleric, 2 Level 4 cleric guards, 8 quaggoth bearers; yield or be stopped for inspection)
City herald with escort (1 herald, 4 fighters HD 3+3; delivering house summons or Council orders; reads aloud in public)
Drow dueling (2 drow fighters HD 3+3; formal challenge, bystanders watching; Black Blade city watch will arrive in 1d6 rounds to stop it)
Street market crowd (20–40 drow civilians, numerous non-drow slaves and traders; pickpockets, Thalmyr informants; chaotic cover for movement)
Noble on palanquin (1 drow noble HD 5+5, 2 bodyguard fighters HD 7+7, 6 quaggoth bearers, 1 Thalmyr spy following at distance)
Mercenary band (6–12 bugbears HD 3+1, 2–4 quaggoths HD 3+3, 1 drow overseer HD 3+3; hired blades between contracts, surly)
City guard patrol — House Xilrath Black Blades (8 elite fighters HD 3+3 AC 1 poisoned weapons, 1 sergeant HD 5+5; checking papers; XPV 200/450 each)
Escaped slave (1–3 mixed humanoids, panicked and lost; 50% chance Thalmyr agents are in pursuit 1d4 rounds behind)
Demon escort (1 Type I demon HD 8 hp 52, 2 Shadowfire mage handlers Level 5; Veythrae business — do not interfere)
Zhaelor inquisitor patrol (4 zealot fighters HD 3+3, 1 cleric Level 6 with full spells; demanding papers and checking slave brands on all passersby)
Velouryn trade caravan (2–3 merchants, 4 Venom Syndicate enforcers HD 3+3, 10–20 slave porters, 2–4 pack lizards; moving through quickly)
Drow youth gang (4–8 young drow fighters HD 2, associated with a minor house; hazing trespassers in their territory, will back down from obvious strength)
Shadowfire battle-mage patrol — House Veythrae (4 battle-mages Level 5 with wands of magic missiles, 1 Archmage Level 8 in robes; conducting surveillance)
